title: MP Study Abroad Scholarship 2026

detail.title: MP Study Abroad Scholarship 2026
detail.short_info: Apply for MP Study Abroad Scholarship 2026 for SC students of Madhya Pradesh to study Master’s & PhD abroad. Check eligibility, amount, last date, and online form.

content.text: MP Study Abroad Scholarship 2026 is a Madhya Pradesh government overseas scholarship scheme for SC category PG (Master’s) and PhD students of MP to study in top‑ranked foreign universities, covering tuition fees, living allowance, contingency and travel support for the 2026 intake.

The Government of Madhya Pradesh has launched the MP Study Abroad Scholarship 2026 (Overseas Scholarship Scheme) to support meritorious Scheduled Caste (SC) students of the state in pursuing Master’s and PhD programmes in reputed foreign universities. This scheme focuses on candidates who secure admission in institutions listed in leading global rankings (such as QS or Times) and who come from families with limited financial capacity. The scholarship aims to reduce the economic barrier for talented students so they can access high‑quality international education and research opportunities. It combines tuition fee support with maintenance, contingency and travel assistance, making overseas study more affordable for eligible students from MP.
Direct Link: Click Here

rows: MP Study Abroad Scholarship Scheme 2026 (Overseas Scholarship for SC students)
Department of Higher Education, Government of Madhya Pradesh
Scheduled Caste (SC) students who are permanent residents of Madhya Pradesh
Admissions generally falling in the 2026 intake (as per official notification)
Online application through the Higher Education / State Scholarship portal
As per latest notification (students must check current last date before applying)
Limited number of students selected each year (merit‑ and criteria‑based)
Tuition fee (subject to cap), maintenance allowance, contingency, travel and related support

content.text: This section targets informational intent and helps readers quickly understand why the scheme matters.

items: State‑level overseas scholarship dedicated to SC students from Madhya Pradesh.
Focus on postgraduate and doctoral studies in foreign universities recognised in global rankings.
Financial coverage designed to handle major cost heads: tuition, living expenses, study materials and travel.
Online application process through the official Higher Education / Scholarship portal.
Limited seats each year, with selection based on eligibility, academic merit and institutional quality.

content.text: You can optionally highlight that the scholarship encourages global exposure, advanced research and skill development among socially and economically disadvantaged students of MP.

sections.heading: MP Study Abroad Scholarship 2026 Eligibility Criteria

content.text: Eligibility is one of the most searched parts of any scholarship article. Present it clearly with concise bullets.
Academic and Course Eligibility

items: The applicant must be planning to pursue, or must have secured admission in, a Master’s (PG) or PhD programme in a foreign university.
The course should be full‑time and recognised by the relevant authority in the host country.

Preference may be given to disciplines with strong research or professional value (you can mention common fields like engineering, management, social sciences, etc., but avoid claiming any strict list unless given officially).

content.text: Category and Domicile

items: The candidate must belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category as recognised by the Government of Madhya Pradesh.
A valid caste certificate issued by the competent authority in MP is mandatory at the time of application.
The applicant must be a permanent resident (domicile) of Madhya Pradesh and should hold a valid MP domicile certificate.

content.text: Age and Other Conditions

items: There is usually a prescribed upper age limit at the time of application (for example mid‑30s); candidates should confirm the exact age criteria in the current year’s notification.

The applicant must have a strong academic record in previous qualifying degrees (UG for Master’s, PG for PhD), typically satisfying minimum percentage or grade requirements specified in the guidelines.
Applicants should not be availing overlapping full scholarships from another major government scheme for the same course, unless explicitly allowed.

content.text: Family Income Criteria

items: The annual family income is subject to an upper limit (for example, many such schemes fix it around a few lakh rupees per year).
Income must be certified by a competent authority through a valid income certificate.
If both parents are working, total income from all sources is counted for eligibility.

content.text: In your live article, you should replace placeholders (like “for example”) with exact numbers and conditions from the current official notification, and clearly mention the source as the Higher Education Department.
This section is crucial for transactional intent, as users want to know “how much” they can get.
Tuition Fee Support

items: The scheme covers either the full approved tuition fee or up to a specified maximum amount per year, whichever is lower.
Tuition fee support is generally provided directly to the foreign university or as per the disbursement mechanism mentioned in official rules.
Only tuition components recognised as academic fee by the institution are usually covered; penalty, late fee or unrelated charges are typically excluded.

content.text: Maintenance Allowance

items: Selected students receive an annual or periodic maintenance allowance meant for living costs such as accommodation, food, local transport and basic personal expenses.
The maintenance amount may be fixed in foreign currency (for example, a set number of USD per year) and may be released in instalments across the duration of the course.
Students must maintain satisfactory academic progress and follow the host university’s regulations to continue receiving the allowance.

content.text: Contingency and Other Academic Support

items: A separate contingency allowance may be provided to cover books, study materials, laboratory fees, thesis preparation and similar academic needs.
This amount is usually smaller than the maintenance allowance but can significantly reduce the out‑of‑pocket academic expenses for the scholar.

content.text: Travel and Additional Allowances

items: The scheme usually covers economy‑class airfare from India to the country of study at the time of joining and return travel after completion, subject to rules.
Visa fees and certain mandatory charges associated with joining the course may also be reimbursed or supported under the scheme guidelines.
Some rules may restrict repeated travel or additional personal trips, so scholars should plan travel in line with policy.

content.text: When you publish, align the amounts and terms precisely with the latest scheme document or government order.
This section addresses user queries like “Is my university eligible?”

items: The scholarship generally targets foreign universities that appear within a specified range in accepted global rankings (commonly QS World University Rankings or Times World University Rankings).
Only universities falling within the specified rank band (for example, top 500) are counted for eligibility; students should verify their chosen university’s latest ranking before applying.
Most major destinations such as the USA, UK, Canada, Europe, Australia and others can be eligible, provided the specific university meets the ranking criteria.
Courses must be recognised, full‑time and equivalent to Master’s or PhD programmes; short‑term diplomas or purely online / distance programmes are typically not covered.

content.text: To add more value, you can include a short example: “For instance, if you receive an offer from a university ranked within the top 300 globally in QS rankings, your course is more likely to meet the ranking requirement, subject to all other conditions.”

sections.heading: Documents Required for MP Study Abroad Scholarship 2026

content.text: This checklist targets transactional intent and helps candidates prepare before starting the online form.

items: Caste certificate (SC) issued by the competent authority in Madhya Pradesh.
Domicile (residence) certificate of Madhya Pradesh.
Income certificate showing total annual family income within the prescribed limit.
Mark sheets and degree certificates of all previous qualifying examinations (10th, 12th, UG, PG as applicable).
Valid passport (first and last page scan) of the applicant.
Admission letter / offer letter from the foreign university, clearly mentioning the course, duration and intake.
Recent passport‑size photograph in the specified size and format.
Scanned signature of the applicant.
Any additional documents mentioned in the current year’s notification (like a brief statement of purpose, undertaking, or bond if required).

content.text: Advise users to keep soft copies (PDF/JPEG) of all documents within the size limit to avoid upload issues on the portal.

sections.heading: How to Apply Online for MP Study Abroad Scholarship 2026

content.text: This is your core “how to” section and should be detailed but clean.
Step‑by‑Step Registration on the Official Portal

items: Visit the official website of the Department of Higher Education, Government of Madhya Pradesh (highereducation.mp.gov.in).
Look for the link related to “Study Abroad Scholarship / Overseas Scholarship” or for scholarship schemes leading to the state scholarship portal.
If you are a new user, complete the registration process by entering your basic details such as name, mobile number, Samagra ID (if required), email ID and other requested information.
Verify your mobile number or email using the OTP or verification link sent by the system.
Create your login credentials (User ID and password) and keep them safely for future logins.

content.text: Filling the MP Study Abroad Scholarship Online Form

items: Log in to the portal using your registered User ID and password.
Navigate to the list of scholarship schemes and select the option related to “MP Study Abroad Scholarship 2026” or “Overseas Scholarship”.
Fill in personal details such as name, date of birth, gender, category, address and contact information correctly as per your documents.
Enter academic details including marks, degrees obtained, year of passing and education board/university names.
Provide details of your foreign admission - country, university name, course title, discipline, duration of the programme and expected date of joining.
Enter family income information exactly as mentioned on your income certificate.
Upload the scanned copies of all required documents within the specified file size and format limits.
Review all entries carefully before final submission to avoid rejection due to errors or mismatches.

content.text: Final Submission and Printout

items: After verifying all details, click on the final “Submit” or “Lock Application” button as instructed on the portal.
Once submitted, download or print the application form and acknowledgement receipt for your records.
If the scheme requires verification by an institute or authority in India, ensure you follow any additional offline/online steps mentioned in the instructions.
Keep checking the portal and your registered email / mobile number for updates, queries or defect notices, and respond promptly if any clarification is requested.
After the application deadline, the concerned department scrutinises all submitted applications for eligibility, completeness and authenticity of documents.
Eligible candidates may be shortlisted based on academic merit, ranking of the foreign institution, category and availability of slots under the scheme.
A final list of selected candidates is generally published on the official website or communicated individually via email/SMS.
Selected students may have to complete certain formalities such as declarations, undertakings or bond forms before the scholarship is finally sanctioned.
Disbursement of scholarship funds (tuition, maintenance and others) follows the procedures laid down by the department and may be linked to joining the course and periodic progress reports.

content.text: In your article, remind users that selection is competitive and that meeting minimum eligibility does not guarantee selection.

items: Primary official website: Department of Higher Education, Government of Madhya Pradesh - Click here
Applicants should refer only to current‑year notifications, PDFs and guidelines available on the official site or the designated scholarship portal.
For clarification, candidates can contact the department through the official helpline numbers or email IDs mentioned in the latest notification.
It is strongly recommended not to rely solely on social media posts, third‑party blogs or coaching websites for final decisions; always cross‑check with official documents.

content.text: You can place internal links on your site (e.g., to related MP scholarships, central overseas scholarships, etc.) to improve user retention and topical authority.
